Meal planning, minus the planning

Come home to dinner that's already decided.

Forkcast picks a week of dinners that quietly share ingredients, then hands you one aisle-sorted grocery list with the price up front, a 90-minute Sunday prep plan, and the cheapest store near you. You keep the veto.

It was never the cooking.

It's the deciding. Fourteen dinner decisions a week — all at 6 p.m., all on you, all when you've got nothing left. That's the chore. So that's the part we took.

How it works

Four things show up. Every week.

It finds the overlap.

The engine chooses dinners that quietly reuse the same ingredients — so one pack stretches across the whole week instead of rotting in the drawer.

One pack of chicken thighs → 3 dinners

One list, sorted by aisle.

Everything you need on a single grocery run, grouped the way the store is laid out — with the total shown before you ever check out.

16 items · $51.81

Ninety minutes, once.

A Sunday prep plan that batches the shared parts — proteins, bases, sauces — so weeknight “cooking” is really just ten minutes of assembly.

~90 min Sunday · ~10 min nights

The cheapest store near you.

Set your location once. Forkcast prices your exact haul at every grocery store within 15 miles and points you to the one that rings up cheapest.

Compared within 15 miles

The whole trick

One pack. Three dinners.

The secret to a cheap week isn't more recipes — it's overlap. Forkcast builds the week around ingredients that show up again and again, so a single chicken thighs pack quietly becomes three different dinners instead of three separate shops.

Fewer ingredients · less waste · lower bill

A whole week, start to finish

Here's the kind of week it makes.

5 overlapping dinners, one list, one prep — the whole payoff on one screen. Open the planner and the numbers update to your kitchen.

Sunday prep

~90 min, once
  1. 15m

    Mise en place

    Wash and chop the shared produce — onion, garlic, peppers, spinach — and store it grab-and-go.

  2. 30m

    Batch-cook proteins

    Cook the chicken thighs and sausage in batches; cool and store, ready to flavor per recipe.

  3. 30m

    Cook bases & sauces

    Cook the jasmine rice and penne; build the curry and tomato sauce bases.

  4. 15m

    Portion & store

    Portion everything into containers, labeled by night. Weeknights are now ~10-minute assembly.

Free. Runs right here in your browser.

The math

Meal kits solved dinner. For about twelve dollars a plate.

A meal kit, per plate
~$12

Boxes, ice packs, shipping, and a subscription that piles up by the door.

Forkcast, per plate
~$5.18

The same solved dinner — at your own store, at grocery prices. Software, not shipping.

No cardboard. No ice packs. No box to cancel. Just the deciding part, done — and the savings stay with you. That's the whole difference.

Why it feels different

Built for a tired Tuesday. Not a perfect one.

It decides. You keep the veto.

Don't like a meal? Swap it, and the list and the prep recalculate around your choice.

Easy, fast, cheap, good.

No guilt, no macros, no “clean eating.” We removed a chore — we didn't add a diet.

Your store, your savings.

Software, not boxes. You shop the prices you already pay, at the cheapest store near you, and pocket the difference.

One good answer.

Not ten thousand recipes at the exact moment you can't make one more decision. Just dinner, handled.

Plan once. Eat all week.
